item.title: SpaceX Holder Says Orbital Data-Centers Offer Big Payoff, Risks
item.description: SpaceX’s $1.75 trillion initial public offering highlights the potential upside — and downside — from Elon Musk’s bold vision to place data centers in Earth orbit, according to one of the company’s private investors.

item.title: Ashley’s Frasers Offers to Buy Hugo Boss in $3.1 Billion Deal
item.description: Frasers Group Plc offered to buy Hugo Boss AG in a deal valuing the German fashion brand at about €2.7 billion ($3.1 billion), as billionaire Mike Ashley seeks to add another well-known label to his growing collection.

item.title: Oil Crunch Is Stoking Asia’s Demand for Coal, Shipping CEO Says
item.description: Coal is emerging as an increasingly attractive alternative to oil for energy-starved countries across Asia, according to Stamatis Tsantanis, chief executive officer of Seanergy Maritime Holdings Corp.

item.title: ASML to Cut Fewer Jobs Than Planned After Union Negotiations
item.description: Chip-equipment maker ASML Holding NV will cut fewer jobs than planned, with redundancies not expected to take effect until May 1, following negotiations with Dutch trade unions.

item.title: Trump Says US Won’t Renew USMCA, Signaling Yearly Reviews
item.description: President Donald Trump said he would not reauthorize the US’s trade deal with Canada and Mexico, setting the stage for months or years of negotiations over provisions governing automobiles and other key industries.

item.title: Russia Restores Access to Roblox Gaming Platform, Interfax Says
item.description: Russia restored access to Roblox Corp.’s gaming platform after concluding the company had complied with local legal requirements, according to Interfax.

item.title: Tether Backs German Robotics Startup Neura in $1.4 Billion Round
item.description: German startup Neura Robotics has raised about $1.4 billion in a funding round backed by stablecoin issuer Tether Holdings SA to develop and ship its artificial intelligence-powered humanoid robot.

item.title: Canadian Bonds Rally After BOC Holds Rates, Cites Weak Economy
item.description: Canadian government bonds rallied across the curve after the Bank of Canada held its policy interest rate steady and Governor Tiff Macklem described the economy as “weak.”

item.title: Apollo’s Zito Says Too Much AI Spending Is for ‘Low IQ’ Tasks
item.description: Apollo Global Management’s John Zito said too many companies are deploying artificial intelligence tools for relatively mundane tasks that don’t justify the significant costs and computing power.

item.title: Signal’s Whittaker Threatens UK Exit Amid Phone-Screening Plan
item.description: Meredith Whittaker, president of encrypted messaging company Signal, has once again threatened to pull the app from the UK over the government’s approach to tech regulation.

item.title: Anthropic CEO Doesn’t Know If Claude Used in Iran School Strike
item.description: Anthropic PBC’s boss said he doesn’t know what role his artificial intelligence model played in a missile strike that killed an estimated 120 children at an elementary school in Iran, reflecting a broader knowledge gap for AI executives who are increasingly selling advanced AI tools to the US military.

item.title: JPMorgan Sees May as CPI ‘High-Water Mark’ With Fed Set to Hold
item.description: JPMorgan Asset Management’s David Kelly predicted the Federal Reserve will decide that the latest inflation data aren’t worrisome enough to prompt policymakers to do anything other than stand pat on interest rates next week.

item.title: Trump’s Pick for BLS Pledges to Maintain Data Agency’s Integrity
item.description: Brett Matsumoto, President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Bureau of Labor Statistics, told lawmakers he was committed to maintaining the integrity and independence of the agency.

item.title: Subprime Auto Dealer America’s Car-Mart Seeks Rescue Financing
item.description: America’s Car-Mart Inc., a used car seller and subprime lender, is working on an eleventh hour capital raise to stave off a potential bankruptcy filing after a cash crunch put the company on the verge of default, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Commerzbank Says It Raised More ‘Concerns’ Tied to UniCredit Bid
item.description: Commerzbank AG expressed fresh doubts whether the rising acceptance level of UniCredit SpA’s takeover bid is a full reflection of investor support.

item.title: Roblox Wants Deluge of Child Sex Abuse Cases Moved Out of Court
item.description: Myra’s daughter got a Roblox account when she was 8. For more than a decade, the immersive gaming platform had advertised itself as a safe place for kids to play, and during the pandemic, there weren’t many other options.

item.title: South Africa’s SARB Sees Another Rate Hike This Year Due to War
item.description: South Africa’s central bank suggested another interest-rate increase could be warranted this year because of the fallout from the Iran war, while highlighting risks the conflict poses to financial stability.

item.title: Amazon Inks $17.5 Billion Loan in Financing Led by Citigroup
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. has inked a $17.5 billion loan from a group of banks led by Citigroup Inc., the tech company said in a regulatory filing.

item.title: Bank of Canada Holds at 2.25% and Warns of Policy ‘Dilemma’
item.description: The Bank of Canada held its key interest rate but reiterated that US trade uncertainty and the Iran war may mean it needs to either cut or deliver consecutive hikes to keep inflation stable.

item.title: Man Accused of Igniting Massive Los Angeles Fire Goes on Trial
item.description: The man accused of “maliciously” starting the most destructive wildfire in Los Angeles history last year is set to face a jury over arson charges.

item.title: Here Are the Key Takeaways From the US CPI Report for May
item.description: Here are the key takeaways on the US consumer price index report for May, released Wednesday:

item.title: Here Comes the Age of the Rockstar CFO
item.description: Financial decision making has never been more strategic.

item.title: Pimco Says ‘Credit Loss Cycle’ Has Begun, Favors Quality Bonds
item.description: Pacific Investment Management Co. is warning that the “credit loss cycle is upon us” as heavy spending on artificial intelligence could widen economic outcomes and hit lower-quality borrowers.

item.title: Brazil Central Bank Seeks Autonomy to Protect Pix, Oversight
item.description: Brazil’s central bank is facing pushback from President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva’s allies and some of its own employees as it seeks greater budget and staffing control, saying resource constraints are hampering oversight of the country’s increasingly complex financial system.

item.title: KKR’s Agrawal Says Market May Be Underestimating AI Power Needs
item.description: AI’s furious pace of growth means power demands could be much higher than people realize, according to KKR & Co.’s Raj Agrawal.

item.title: Trump Media, TAE No Longer Pursuing Truth Social Spinoff
item.description: Trump Media & Technology Group, TAE Technologies Inc. and Texas Ventures Acquisition III have decided not to continue pursuing a spinoff of some media assets, including Truth Social.

item.title: Sweden’s Top Pension Gatekeeper Wants to Keep Private Credit Out
item.description: The top official deciding how Swedes invest much of their pension savings said he’s against offering them access to private markets funds.

item.title: Microsoft Has an Answer to Anti-AI Boos From the Class of 2026
item.description: In recent weeks, the Class of 2026 has made its displeasure with AI abundantly clear, loudly booing speakers touting the technology during commencement speeches.

item.title: Argentine Peso Drops Most Over Eight Weeks Since Midterm Selloff
item.description: An incipient decline in the Argentine peso is gaining momentum, as investors grow more risk averse and policymakers in Buenos Aires loosen their grip on the volatile currency.

item.title: CIBC to Offer SpaceX Access Through Canadian Depositary Receipt
item.description: Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce is set to offer Canadian investors access to SpaceX through a vehicle that gives local investors access to global stocks.
